content_en:
The polemical dimensions of At-Tamhid are integral to its purpose and structure. Al-Baqillani systematically addresses the positions of multiple groups that he regards as having departed from correct Islamic theology, showing in each case where their reasoning goes wrong and how the Ash'ari position avoids those errors.

His engagement with the Mu'tazila is the most extensive, reflecting their status as the primary theological opposition at the time. He addresses Mu'tazili positions on the divine attributes (which they reduced to the divine essence, denying real distinct attributes), on divine justice (which they made rationally obligatory), on the createdness of the Quran, on the efficacy of human agency, and on the impossibility of seeing God in the afterlife. In each case, he shows both that the Mu'tazili position conflicts with the Quran and Sunnah and that it contains rational errors or false premises.

His engagement with the anthropomorphists (mushabbihah) is equally important for understanding the Ash'ari position. The Ash'ari school defined itself against two opposing errors: the ta'til (denial) of the Mu'tazila, who stripped God of real attributes, and the tashbih (likening) of anthropomorphists, who understood divine attributes in a way that made them resemble created attributes. By refuting both extremes, At-Tamhid clarifies the middle position that defines Ash'ari theology.

Al-Baqillani also engages with philosophers (falasifah), Kharijites, and various sectarian positions. His engagement with philosophical challenges is particularly significant for the development of the Ash'ari school's intellectual defense. The philosophers challenged Islamic theology on questions of the eternity of the world, the nature of divine knowledge (whether God knows particulars), and the resurrection of bodies. Al-Baqillani addresses these challenges using the kalam tools the school had developed, building the foundation that al-Ghazali would later develop into the more systematic Tahafut al-Falasifah.

The breadth of the work's polemical engagement reflects al-Baqillani's understanding of the Ash'ari school's mission: to defend correct Islamic theology against all challengers from whatever direction they come, using the tools of reason and the evidence of scripture and tradition. This comprehensive defensive project required engaging seriously with diverse opponents, and At-Tamhid demonstrates that engagement at a high level of intellectual rigor.
content_id:
Dimensi-dimensi polemis At-Tamhid merupakan bagian integral dari tujuan dan strukturnya. Al-Baqillani secara sistematis menangani posisi-posisi banyak kelompok yang ia anggap telah menyimpang dari teologi Islam yang benar, menunjukkan dalam setiap kasus di mana penalaran mereka salah dan bagaimana posisi Asy'ariyyah menghindari kesalahan-kesalahan tersebut.

Keterlibatannya dengan Mu'tazilah adalah yang paling luas, mencerminkan status mereka sebagai oposisi teologis utama pada saat itu. Ia menangani posisi-posisi Mu'tazilah tentang sifat-sifat Ilahi (yang mereka reduksi menjadi Dzat Ilahi, menolak sifat-sifat yang nyata dan berbeda), tentang keadilan Ilahi (yang mereka jadikan secara rasional wajib), tentang kemakhlukan Al-Quran, tentang efektivitas agen manusia, dan tentang ketidakmungkinan melihat Allah di akhirat. Dalam setiap kasus, ia menunjukkan bahwa posisi Mu'tazilah bertentangan dengan Al-Quran dan Sunnah dan bahwa posisi tersebut mengandung kesalahan rasional atau premis-premis yang salah.

Keterlibatannya dengan kaum antropomorfis (musyabbihah) sama pentingnya untuk memahami posisi Asy'ariyyah. Mazhab Asy'ariyyah mendefinisikan dirinya melawan dua kesalahan yang berlawanan: ta'til (penolakan) dari Mu'tazilah, yang menelanjangi Allah dari sifat-sifat yang nyata, dan tasybieh (penyerupaan) dari kaum antropomorfis, yang memahami sifat-sifat Ilahi dengan cara yang menjadikannya menyerupai sifat-sifat makhluk. Dengan membantah kedua ekstrem tersebut, At-Tamhid mengklarifikasi posisi tengah yang mendefinisikan teologi Asy'ariyyah.

Al-Baqillani juga terlibat dengan para filsuf (falasifah), Khawarij, dan berbagai posisi sektarian. Keterlibatannya dengan tantangan-tantangan filosofis sangat signifikan bagi pengembangan pertahanan intelektual mazhab Asy'ariyyah. Para filsuf menantang teologi Islam pada pertanyaan-pertanyaan tentang keabadian dunia, hakikat pengetahuan Ilahi (apakah Allah mengetahui hal-hal partikular), dan kebangkitan jasad. Al-Baqillani menangani tantangan-tantangan ini menggunakan perangkat-perangkat kalam yang telah dikembangkan oleh mazhab, membangun fondasi yang kemudian dikembangkan oleh al-Ghazali menjadi Tahafut al-Falasifah yang lebih sistematis.

Keluasan keterlibatan polemis karya ini mencerminkan pemahaman al-Baqillani tentang misi mazhab Asy'ariyyah: membela teologi Islam yang benar dari semua penantang dari arah manapun mereka datang, menggunakan perangkat akal dan bukti kitab suci serta tradisi. Proyek defensif yang komprehensif ini membutuhkan keterlibatan serius dengan berbagai lawan, dan At-Tamhid menunjukkan keterlibatan itu pada tingkat ketelitian intelektual yang tinggi.
content_ar: The polemical dimensions of At-Tamhid are integral to its purpose and structure. Al-Baqillani systematically addresses the positions of multiple groups that he regards as having departed from correct Islamic theology, showing in each case where their reasoning goes الخطأ and how the الأشعري position avoids those errors. His engagement with the المعتزلة is the most extensive, reflecting their status as the الأساسي theological opposition at the time. He addresses Mu'tazili positions on the divine attributes (which they reduced to the divine essence, denying real distinct attributes), on divine العدل (which they made rationally obligatory), on the createdness of the القرآن الكريم, on the efficacy of human agency, and on the impossibility of seeing God in the الآخرة. In each case, he shows both that the Mu'tazili position conflicts with the القرآن الكريم and السنة and that it contains rational errors or false premises. His engagement with the anthropomorphists (mushabbihah) is equally المهم for الفهم the الأشعري position. The الأشعري school defined itself against two opposing errors: the ta'til (denial) of the المعتزلة, who stripped God of real attributes, and the tashbih (likening) of anthropomorphists, who understood divine attributes in a الطريق that made them resemble created attributes. By refuting both extremes, At-Tamhid clarifies the middle position that defines الأشعري theology. Al-Baqillani أيضاً engages with philosophers (falasifah), Kharijites, and various sectarian positions. His engagement with philosophical challenges is particularly المهم for the development of the الأشعري school's intellectual defense. The philosophers challenged Islamic theology on questions of the eternity of the الدنيا, the nature of divine العلم (whether God knows particulars), and the البعث of bodies. Al-Baqillani addresses these challenges using the علم الكلام tools the school had developed, building the foundation that al-Ghazali would later develop into the more systematic Tahafut al-Falasifah. The breadth of the work's polemical engagement reflects al-Baqillani's الفهم of the الأشعري school's mission: to defend correct Islamic theology against all challengers from whatever direction they come, using the tools of العقل and the evidence of scripture and التراث. This comprehensive defensive project required engaging seriously with diverse opponents, and At-Tamhid demonstrates that engagement at a high level of intellectual rigor.
